title: Early Norepinephrine in Trauma Patients With Hemorrhagic Shock

skipped_criteria: Inclusion #2a/2b: Significant bleeding evidence (exsanguinous external bleeding ≥500 ml, FAST positive, hemothorax, CT bleeding) — no schema field for bleeding volume, FAST result, or imaging findings; partially covered by presumed_hemorrhage.
Inclusion #3a: Mean arterial pressure < 65 mmHg — no MAP field in schema.
Inclusion #3a: Decrease in blood pressure from baseline > 30 mmHg — no baseline BP comparison field in schema.
Inclusion #3b: Capillary refill time > 2 seconds — no schema field for capillary refill.
Inclusion #3b: Base excess < -6 mEq/L — no schema field for base excess.
Inclusion #3b: Lactate level > 2 mmol/L — no schema field for lactate.
Exclusion #1: Prehospital CPR or CPR at trauma bay — no schema field for CPR history; cardiac_arrest mechanism is not equivalent.
Exclusion #2: Persistent shock for more than 12 hours prior to randomization — no schema field for shock duration.
Exclusion #4: Traumatic limb injury with acute limb ischemia — no schema field for limb ischemia.
Exclusion #5: History of limb ischemia, chronic heart disease, chronic lung disease, or chronic renal disease — no schema field for prior medical history.
Exclusion #7: Documented DNR order or declined life-sustaining intervention — no schema field for code status.

Exclusion #8: Allergy to norepinephrine or contraindications (peripheral vascular thrombosis, mesenteric thrombosis, fatal tachyarrhythmia) — no schema field for drug allergies or vascular conditions.
Exclusion #9: Current use of ergotamine, MAOIs, or TCAs — no schema field for medication use.
Exclusion #10: Prior vasopressor administration before randomization — no schema field for prior vasopressor use.
Exclusion #11: Weight less than 30 kg — no schema field for patient weight.
